mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates robust aggregate demand and healthy business investment, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reflects expanding economic activity, which historically correlates with higher corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when GDP growth is above trend, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite sustained inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirm a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, providing a fundamental tailwind for equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative or flat forward returns during periods of strong payroll growth, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when industrial production falls for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive R-squared is below 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment spending reflects tightening credit conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite significant drops in residential investment, or the signal fails to generate negative forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year test.
